3: use crate::cmd::mutation_guard;

11: #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
12: pub fn run(
13:     draft: bool,
14:     no_draft: bool,
15:     no_pr: bool,
16:     title: Option<&str>,
17:     body: Option<&str>,
18:     body_file: Option<&str>,
19:     base_override: Option<&str>,
20:     stack: bool,
21:     stage_all: bool,
22:     stage_all_files: bool,
23:     commit_message: Option<&str>,
24:     repo_override: Option<&str>,
25:     remote_override: Option<&str>,
26: ) -> Result<()> {
27:     if stack {
28:         return crate::cmd::submit::run(draft, no_draft, title, body, body_file, repo_override, remote_override);
29:     }
30: 
31:     if let Some(root) = git::current_linked_worktree_root()? {
32:         ui::linked_worktree_warning(&root);
33:     }
34: 
35:     let mut commit_scope_defined = false;
36:     let mut commit_scope_mode: Option<String> = None;
37:     let mut commit_out_of_scope_files: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
38: 
39:     // If -a or -m was provided, do the commit first.
40:     if stage_all || stage_all_files || commit_message.is_some() {
41:         if let Some(msg) = commit_message {
42:             let stage_mode = if stage_all_files {
43:                 Some(StageMode::All)
44:             } else if stage_all {
45:                 Some(StageMode::Tracked)
46:             } else {
47:                 None
48:             };
49:             let outcome = mutation_guard::commit_with_guard(msg, stage_mode, false, &[])?
50:                 .expect("commit_with_guard returns Some when --if-changed is false");
51:             commit_scope_defined = outcome.scope.scope_defined;
52:             commit_scope_mode = outcome.scope.scope_mode.clone();
53:             commit_out_of_scope_files = outcome.scope.out_of_scope_files.clone();
54:             let current = outcome.current;
55:             ui::info(&format!("Committed on `{current}`: {msg}"));
56: 
57:             if let Ok(stat) = git::show_stat_head() {
58:                 let stat = stat.trim();
59:                 if !stat.is_empty() {
60:                     eprintln!("{stat}");
61:                 }
62:             }
63: 
64:             // Restack children (same as ez commit).
65:             let state = StackState::load()?;
66:             if state.is_managed(&current) {
67:                 let children = state.children_of(&current);
68:                 if !children.is_empty() {
69:                     crate::cmd::restack::run(false)?;
70:                 }
71:             }
72:         }
73:     }
74: 
75:     let mut state = StackState::load()?;
76:     let current = git::current_branch()?;
77: 
78:     if state.is_trunk(&current) {
79:         bail!(EzError::OnTrunk);
80:     }
81: 
82:     if !state.is_managed(&current) {
83:         bail!(EzError::BranchNotInStack(current.clone()));
84:     }
85: 
86:     let remote = match remote_override {
87:         Some(r) => r.to_string(),
88:         None => state.effective_push_remote(&current),
89:     };
90: 
91:     // Resolve --no-pr: flag > config > false
92:     let skip_pr = no_pr || state.no_pr.unwrap_or(false);
93: 
94:     // Resolve draft: --draft/--no-draft flags > config > false
95:     let effective_draft = if no_draft {
96:         false
97:     } else if draft {
98:         true
99:     } else {
100:         state.draft.unwrap_or(false)
101:     };
102: 
103:     let resolved_body: Option<String> = match body_file {
104:         Some(path) => Some(github::body_from_file(path)?),
105:         None => body.map(|s| s.to_string()),
106:     };
107: 
108:     let parent = if let Some(b) = base_override {
109:         b.to_string()
110:     } else {
111:         state.get_branch(&current)?.parent.clone()
112:     };
113: 
114:     // Push the branch with force-with-lease.
115:     let sp = ui::spinner(&format!("Pushing `{current}`..."));
116:     git::fetch_branch(&remote, &current)?;
117:     git::push(&remote, &current, true)?;
118:     sp.finish_and_clear();
119:     ui::info(&format!("Pushed `{current}`"));
120: 
121:     // Store push_remote when --remote was explicitly passed on the CLI.
122:     if let Some(r) = remote_override {
123:         state.get_branch_mut(&current)?.push_remote = Some(r.to_string());
124:     }
125: 
126:     if skip_pr {
127:         state.save()?;
128:         ui::success(&format!("Pushed `{current}` (no PR)"));
129:         ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
130:             "cmd": "push",
131:             "branch": current,
132:             "no_pr": true,
133:             "scope_defined": commit_scope_defined,
134:             "scope_mode": commit_scope_mode,
135:             "out_of_scope_count": commit_out_of_scope_files.len(),
136:             "out_of_scope_files": commit_out_of_scope_files,
137:         }));
138:         return Ok(());
139:     }
140: 
141:     let body_explicitly_set = body.is_some() || body_file.is_some();
142: 
143:     // Create or update the PR.
144:     let had_pr_before = state
145:         .get_branch(&current)
146:         .ok()
147:         .and_then(|m| m.pr_number)
148:         .is_some();
149: 
150:     let pr_url = push_or_update_pr(
151:         &mut state,
152:         &current,
153:         &parent,
154:         effective_draft,
155:         title,
156:         resolved_body.as_deref(),
157:         body_explicitly_set,
158:         repo_override,
159:     )?;
160: 
161:     let pr_number = state.get_branch(&current).ok().and_then(|m| m.pr_number);
162: 
163:     state.save()?;
164: 
165:     // Update stack sections on contiguous sibling PRs.
166:     update_contiguous_stack_bodies(&state, &current);
167: 
168:     ui::success(&format!("PR: {pr_url}"));
169: 
170:     ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
171:         "cmd": "push",
172:         "branch": current,
173:         "pr_number": pr_number,
174:         "pr_url": pr_url,
175:         "created": !had_pr_before,
176:         "scope_defined": commit_scope_defined,
177:         "scope_mode": commit_scope_mode,
178:         "out_of_scope_count": commit_out_of_scope_files.len(),
179:         "out_of_scope_files": commit_out_of_scope_files,
180:     }));
181: 
182:     Ok(())
183: }

185: /// Push-or-update logic shared with the `submit` command.
186: ///
187: /// Repo resolution order: `repo_override` (CLI --repo) > `branch.pr_repo` (stored from first push) > `state.repo` (global config) > None.
188: /// When `--repo` is explicitly passed, the value is stored in `branch.pr_repo` so future pushes reuse it.
189: ///
190: /// Returns the PR URL.
191: pub fn push_or_update_pr(
192:     state: &mut StackState,
193:     branch: &str,
194:     parent: &str,
195:     draft: bool,
196:     title_override: Option<&str>,
197:     body_override: Option<&str>,
198:     body_explicitly_set: bool,
199:     repo_override: Option<&str>,
200: ) -> Result<String> {
201:     // Normalize repo shorthand (e.g. "fork" → "owner/repo" from remote URL).
202:     let resolved_override: Option<String> =
203:         repo_override.map(|s| github::resolve_repo_shorthand(s));
204:     // Resolve effective repo: CLI flag > stored per-branch > global config > None
205:     let effective_repo: Option<String> = resolved_override
206:         .clone()
207:         .or_else(|| state.effective_pr_repo(branch));
208: 
209:     // Resolve the push remote for cross-fork head prefix.
210:     let push_remote = state.effective_push_remote(branch);
211: 
212:     let existing_pr = github::get_pr_status_in_repo(branch, effective_repo.as_deref())?;
213: 
214:     let pr_url = match existing_pr {
215:         Some(pr) => {
216:             state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.pr_number = Some(pr.number);
217: 
218:             // Build the full stack tree (PR number already known for existing PRs).
219:             let tree_roots = crate::stack_body::build_full_tree(state, branch);
220:             let stack_tree = crate::stack_body::render_full_tree(&tree_roots);
221: 
222:             // Update PR base only when the stack parent is genuinely an ancestor of this branch.
223:             if pr.base != parent {
224:                 if git::is_ancestor(parent, branch) {
225:                     if let Err(e) = github::update_pr_base_in_repo(
226:                         pr.number,
227:                         parent,
228:                         effective_repo.as_deref(),
229:                     ) {
230:                         ui::warn(&format!(
231:                             "Push succeeded but PR #{} base could not be updated to `{parent}`: {e}",
232:                             pr.number
233:                         ));
234:                     } else {
235:                         ui::info(&format!("Updated PR #{} base to `{parent}`", pr.number));
236:                     }
237:                 } else {
238:                     ui::warn(&format!(
239:                         "PR #{} base not updated: `{parent}` is not an ancestor of `{branch}` \
240:                          (stack metadata may be stale — run `ez sync` or update manually)",
241:                         pr.number
242:                     ));
243:                 }
244:             }
245: 
246:             // Body update logic:
247:             // 1. If user explicitly passed --body/--body-file: build full ez body.
248:             // 2. If existing body has ez markers: regenerate all ez sections.
249:             // 3. If title override only: update title.
250:             if body_explicitly_set {
251:                 let raw_body = body_override.unwrap_or("Part of a stack managed by `ez`.");
252:                 let refs_section = extract_references_for_branch(state, branch, parent);
253:                 let body = crate::stack_body::build_ez_body(
254:                     raw_body,
255:                     Some("Part of a stack managed by `ez`."),
256:                     refs_section.as_deref(),
257:                     &stack_tree,
258:                 );
259:                 if let Err(e) = github::edit_pr_in_repo(
260:                     pr.number,
261:                     title_override,
262:                     Some(&body),
263:                     effective_repo.as_deref(),
264:                 ) {
265:                     ui::warn(&format!(
266:                         "Push succeeded but PR #{} could not be updated: {e}",
267:                         pr.number
268:                     ));
269:                 } else {
270:                     ui::info(&format!("Updated PR #{}", pr.number));
271:                 }
272:             } else {
273:                 // Fetch current body to check for ez markers.
274:                 let current_body = github::get_pr_body_in_repo(pr.number, effective_repo.as_deref())
275:                     .unwrap_or_default();
276:                 if crate::stack_body::has_ez_markers(&current_body) {
277:                     // Regenerate ez sections, preserving user content above markers.
278:                     let parsed = crate::stack_body::parse_ez_body(&current_body);
279:                     let refs_section = if parsed.has_references_markers {
280:                         extract_references_for_branch(state, branch, parent)
281:                     } else {
282:                         None // User removed references subsection — don't regenerate.
283:                     };
284:                     let summary = if parsed.has_summary_markers {
285:                         Some(parsed.summary.as_deref().unwrap_or("Part of a stack managed by `ez`."))
286:                     } else {
287:                         None // User removed summary subsection.
288:                     };
289:                     let body = crate::stack_body::build_ez_body(
290:                         &parsed.user_body,
291:                         summary,
292:                         refs_section.as_deref(),
293:                         &stack_tree,
294:                     );
295:                     if body != current_body {
296:                         if let Err(e) = github::edit_pr_in_repo(
297:                             pr.number,
298:                             title_override,
299:                             Some(&body),
300:                             effective_repo.as_deref(),
301:                         ) {
302:                             ui::warn(&format!(
303:                                 "Push succeeded but PR #{} body could not be updated: {e}",
304:                                 pr.number
305:                             ));
306:                         } else {
307:                             ui::info(&format!("Updated PR #{} body", pr.number));
308:                         }
309:                     } else if title_override.is_some() {
310:                         if let Err(e) =
311:                             github::edit_pr_in_repo(pr.number, title_override, None, effective_repo.as_deref())
312:                         {
313:                             ui::warn(&format!(
314:                                 "Push succeeded but PR #{} title could not be updated: {e}",
315:                                 pr.number
316:                             ));
317:                         }
318:                     }
319:                 } else if title_override.is_some() {
320:                     // No ez markers — only update title if requested.
321:                     if let Err(e) =
322:                         github::edit_pr_in_repo(pr.number, title_override, None, effective_repo.as_deref())
323:                     {
324:                         ui::warn(&format!(
325:                             "Push succeeded but PR #{} title could not be updated: {e}",
326:                             pr.number
327:                         ));
328:                     } else {
329:                         ui::info(&format!("Updated PR #{} title", pr.number));
330:                     }
331:                 }
332:             }
333: 
334:             pr.url
335:         }
336:         None => {
337:             // Derive the PR title from the first commit on this branch.
338:             let range = format!("{parent}..{branch}");
339:             let commits = git::log_oneline(&range, 1)?;
340:             let derived_title = commits
341:                 .first()
342:                 .map(|(_, msg)| msg.clone())
343:                 .unwrap_or_else(|| branch.to_string());
344: 
345:             let title = title_override.unwrap_or(&derived_title);
346:             let user_body = body_override.unwrap_or("");
347: 
348:             // Extract references from commit messages if no explicit body.
349:             let refs_section = extract_references_for_branch(state, branch, parent);
350: 
351:             // Build initial body with stack tree (current branch won't have PR link yet).
352:             let tree_roots = crate::stack_body::build_full_tree(state, branch);
353:             let stack_tree = crate::stack_body::render_full_tree(&tree_roots);
354:             let body = crate::stack_body::build_ez_body(
355:                 user_body,
356:                 Some("Part of a stack managed by `ez`."),
357:                 refs_section.as_deref(),
358:                 &stack_tree,
359:             );
360: 
361:             // Compute cross-fork --head value.
362:             let head = github::cross_fork_head(branch, &push_remote, effective_repo.as_deref());
363: 
364:             let pr = github::create_pr_in_repo(
365:                 title,
366:                 &body,
367:                 parent,
368:                 &head,
369:                 draft,
370:                 effective_repo.as_deref(),
371:             )?;
372:             state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.pr_number = Some(pr.number);
373:             // Only persist pr_repo when --repo was explicitly passed on the CLI.
374:             if let Some(ref r) = resolved_override {
375:                 state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.pr_repo = Some(r.clone());
376:             }
377: 
378:             // Now rebuild the tree with the PR number and update the body.
379:             let tree_roots = crate::stack_body::build_full_tree(state, branch);
380:             let new_stack_tree = crate::stack_body::render_full_tree(&tree_roots);
381:             if new_stack_tree != stack_tree {
382:                 let updated_body = crate::stack_body::build_ez_body(
383:                     user_body,
384:                     Some("Part of a stack managed by `ez`."),
385:                     refs_section.as_deref(),
386:                     &new_stack_tree,
387:                 );
388:                 let _ = github::edit_pr_in_repo(pr.number, None, Some(&updated_body), effective_repo.as_deref());
389:             }
390: 
391:             ui::info(&format!("Created PR #{}: {}", pr.number, pr.url));
392:             pr.url
393:         }
394:     };
395: 
396:     Ok(pr_url)
397: }
398: 
399: /// Extract references from commit messages on a branch.
400: fn extract_references_for_branch(
401:     state: &StackState,
402:     branch: &str,
403:     parent: &str,
404: ) -> Option<String> {
405:     let range = format!("{parent}..{branch}");
406:     let messages = git::log_full_messages(&range).unwrap_or_default();
407:     if messages.is_empty() {
408:         return None;
409:     }
410: 
411:     let extracted = crate::commit_refs::extract_refs_from_messages(&messages);
412:     if extracted.is_empty() {
413:         return None;
414:     }
415: 
416:     // Build stack PR number map for resolving plain #N references.
417:     let mut stack_prs = std::collections::HashMap::new();
418:     for (name, meta) in &state.branches {
419:         if let Some(num) = meta.pr_number {
420:             let url = crate::stack_body::pr_url_for_branch_pub(state, name)
421:                 .unwrap_or_default();
422:             stack_prs.insert(num, (name.clone(), url));
423:         }
424:     }
425: 
426:     // Determine upstream repo for resolving plain #N refs.
427:     let upstream_repo = determine_upstream_repo(state, branch);
428: 
429:     let resolved = crate::commit_refs::resolve_refs(&extracted, &stack_prs, &upstream_repo);
430:     crate::commit_refs::format_references_section(&resolved)
431: }
432: 
433: /// Determine the upstream repo for resolving plain #N references.
434: ///
435: /// Priority: first parent's pr_repo → state.repo → default remote repo.
436: fn determine_upstream_repo(state: &StackState, branch: &str) -> String {
437:     // Walk up to find first ancestor with a pr_repo.
438:     let path = state.path_to_trunk(branch);
439:     for b in &path {
440:         if let Some(meta) = state.branches.get(b.as_str()) {
441:             if let Some(ref repo) = meta.pr_repo {
442:                 return repo.clone();
443:             }
444:         }
445:     }
446:     // Fall back to global repo config.
447:     if let Some(ref repo) = state.repo {
448:         return repo.clone();
449:     }
450:     // Fall back to `gh repo view` default.
451:     github::repo_name().unwrap_or_default()
452: }
453: 
454: /// Update the stack section on all contiguous PRs in the chain.
455: ///
456: /// After pushing a branch, this walks the contiguous PR chain and
457: /// re-renders the stack tree section on each sibling PR.
458: pub fn update_contiguous_stack_bodies(state: &StackState, pushed_branch: &str) {
459:     let chain = crate::stack_body::contiguous_pr_chain(state, pushed_branch);
460: 
461:     for branch_name in &chain {
462:         if branch_name == pushed_branch {
463:             continue; // Already updated during push.
464:         }
465:         let Some(meta) = state.branches.get(branch_name.as_str()) else {
466:             continue;
467:         };
468:         let Some(pr_number) = meta.pr_number else {
469:             continue;
470:         };
471: 
472:         let effective_repo = state.effective_pr_repo(branch_name);
473: 
474:         // Fetch current body.
475:         let current_body = match github::get_pr_body_in_repo(pr_number, effective_repo.as_deref()) {
476:             Ok(b) => b,
477:             Err(_) => continue,
478:         };
479: 
480:         if !crate::stack_body::has_ez_markers(&current_body) {
481:             continue; // No ez markers — skip.
482:         }
483: 
484:         // Build the tree from this branch's perspective.
485:         let tree_roots = crate::stack_body::build_full_tree(state, branch_name);
486:         let new_stack = crate::stack_body::render_full_tree(&tree_roots);
487: 
488:         // Replace only the stack subsection.
489:         if let Some(updated_body) = crate::stack_body::update_stack_section_only(&current_body, &new_stack) {
490:             if updated_body != current_body {
491:                 if let Err(e) = github::edit_pr_in_repo(pr_number, None, Some(&updated_body), effective_repo.as_deref()) {
492:                     ui::warn(&format!(
493:                         "Could not update stack section on PR #{}: {e}",
494:                         pr_number
495:                     ));
496:                 } else {
497:                     ui::info(&format!("Updated stack section on PR #{}", pr_number));
498:                 }
499:             }
500:         }
501:     }
502: }

504: #[cfg(test)]
505: mod tests {

509:     #[test]
510:     fn draft_resolution_no_draft_flag_wins() {
511:         // --no-draft should override both --draft and config
512:         let no_draft = true;
513:         let draft = true;
514:         let config_draft = Some(true);
515: 
516:         let effective = if no_draft {
517:             false
518:         } else if draft {
519:             true
520:         } else {
521:             config_draft.unwrap_or(false)
522:         };
523:         assert!(!effective, "--no-draft should override everything");
524:     }

601:     #[test]
602:     fn repo_override_replaces_stored_and_config() {
603:         // CLI --repo flag > stored pr_repo > config repo
604:         let mut state = StackState::new("main".to_string());
605:         state.repo = Some("config/repo".to_string());
606:         state.add_branch("feat/a", "main", "aaa", None, None);
607:         state.get_branch_mut("feat/a").unwrap().pr_repo = Some("stored/repo".to_string());
608: 
609:         let repo_override: Option<&str> = Some("cli/override");
610:         let effective = repo_override
611:             .map(|s| s.to_string())
612:             .or_else(|| state.effective_pr_repo("feat/a"));

719:     fn pr_repo_not_overwritten_on_update() {
720:         // When a PR already exists, pr_repo should stay unchanged
721:         let mut state = StackState::new("main".to_string());
722:         state.add_branch("feat/a", "main", "aaa", None, None);
723:         state.get_branch_mut("feat/a").unwrap().pr_number = Some(42);
724:         state.get_branch_mut("feat/a").unwrap().pr_repo = Some("original/repo".to_string());
725: 
726:         // push_or_update_pr only sets pr_repo in the None (new PR) branch
727:         // Existing PR path just updates pr_number, doesn't touch pr_repo
728:         assert_eq!(state.get_branch("feat/a").unwrap().pr_repo.as_deref(), Some("original/repo"));
729:     }
